$comment: Generative news-article corpus for the 1970s (glamour-and-grit motorsport-weekly voice — warm, novelistic, present-tense print race reports, cigarette-livery money and jet-set danger, wings and ground-effect arriving late-decade), parsed by Companion.Core.News.NewsArticleBank. Keys under 'bodies' are 'journalPhase|cause'; each holds era-keyed body templates selected deterministically via the 'headlines' PCG32 stream. Bodies compose literal prose, fact tokens, and {pool:name} references. Fact tokens: {player} {team} {race} {year} {round} {position} {expected} {winner} {fieldSize} {champPosition} {champMove} {champLeader}.

1970s: From the paddock at {race}, our correspondent writes.
A dispatch from {race}, in the season of {year}.
Trackside at {race}, where the sponsors' colours blaze.
Filed from the pit lane at {race}, {year}.
By our man among the transporters and the money.
A letter from {race}, where danger and glamour keep their old alliance.
From {race}, {year}, amid the fumes of oil and the scent of money.
Our correspondent sends this from the pit lane at {race}.
Written at {race}, under a sky the colour of pit smoke.
From the paddock at {race}, where the transporters gleam and the stakes run high.
default: Race report from {race}, {year}.
A report from {race}.
Filed from {race}, {year}.
A report from the paddock at {race}.

1970s: In the motorhomes tonight the champagne is on ice, and the season sings.
The paddock, all money and glamour, has found itself a new favourite.
One for the scrapbooks, and for the backers who paid handsomely to be there.
The crowd streams away warmed by what they have seen.
A drive to set the turnstiles clicking at the next round.
Tonight the paddock belongs to the brave, and the champagne to the swift.
The photographers got their picture, and the backers got their money's worth.
A performance to send the sponsors home smiling and the crowd home hoarse.
They will remember this one long after the transporters have rolled away.
The pit garage hums with the happy chaos of a job well done.
default: A fine afternoon's work.
The season carries on.
A result to be pleased with.

1970s: In the transporter there are long faces and longer silences.
The sponsors' guests drift away, their champagne gone flat.
A weekend the team will want washed clean from the record.
There is racing yet to come, and the paddock is nothing if not forgiving.
The stopwatch, cruel and honest, keeps its own counsel.
The mechanics pack away in silence, their overalls dark with a wasted afternoon.
In the pit garage the tools go down heavy, and nobody meets an eye.
The sponsors' hospitality tent empties early, the smiles gone with the sunshine.
There will be words behind closed doors, and a long ride home in the transporter.
Motor racing gives with one hand and takes with the other; today it only took.
default: A day best forgotten.
The team looks ahead to the next round.
Not a day to remember fondly.

So {player} climbs to {champPosition} in the reckoning for the title, {champMove}.
The points ledger has {player} {champPosition} now, {champMove}, {champLeader} the standard by which all are judged.

1970s: the cigarette livery gleaming under the lights
backers' names bright along the flanks
the sponsors' colours worn like a matador's cape
the money men on the pit wall smiling for once
the bodywork ablaze with the season's richest names
the tobacco money glinting on every panel
the flanks a rolling billboard of the season's fashions
the backers' logos catching the low afternoon sun
the livery loud enough to be heard across the paddock
the sponsors' champagne already on ice in the motorhome

the sponsors watching from the pit wall
the backers' names along the bodywork

1970s: the new wings biting hard into the corners
downforce pressing the car into the road as never before
the ground-effect skirts doing their quiet, ferocious work
the aerodynamics rewriting what a corner might be taken at
the wings hauling the car through the quick stuff on rails
the sidepods sealing the machine to the tarmac
the front and rear foils trimmed to a hair and biting hard
the underbody drawing the car earthward through every sweep

the machine well and truly sorted
the car running sweetly all afternoon

the long duel with {winner} lit up the afternoon
{winner} settled the argument out in front

the wheel-to-wheel with {winner} was the story of the day

it was {winner} who would not be denied at the front
{winner} carried the day where the money and the glory meet

{winner} led the way

1970s: The transporters roll for home now, and a long summer of racing folds gently into the record and the memory.
In the motorhomes the corks are pulled and the sponsors' guests linger late, for a season like this one comes but rarely.
The paddock empties, the money men tally their winnings, and already the talk drifts toward next spring and all it might hold.
The garages go dark now, the tyres stacked and cold, and a season's worth of noise and colour settles at last into legend.
Winter closes over the circuits, the money is counted, the vanquished and the victors go their separate ways, and the sport dreams already of spring.
One by one the transporters fire up and pull away into the dusk, carrying home the glory and the grief of a year that gave both in full measure.
The champagne dries on the concrete, the grandstands stand empty, and the long silence between seasons begins its patient work.
default: The season is run, and the paddock turns its face toward winter.
So closes another year of racing, and another begins to take shape.
Another season passes into the record, and the wait for the next begins.

1970s: The season of {year} is run to its very last mile, and it is {player} who stands atop it all — champion of the world, the {team} name gilded now in the record book for as long as men keep such things. It has been a long and glorious summer of racing, of glamour and grease and money by the fistful, and every hard-won yard of it belonged, in the end, to this. {pool:seasonClose}

Champions are not made in a single afternoon but forged across the whole long arc of a season, and the {year} crown, richly and unarguably, is {player}'s. The {team} motorhome will toast this one for years yet, the sponsors' colours never worn more proudly than they are tonight. {pool:seasonClose}

It is done, and it is {player}'s: the {year} World Championship, won not in a day but in a hundred small acts of nerve and cunning across a long and glittering season. The {team} garage will not sleep tonight, nor should it — crowns like this are the work of a lifetime, and this one is earned to the last drop. {pool:seasonClose}

When the history of {year} is written it will carry one name above all others, and that name is {player}. Champion of the world for {team}, and no argument to be had — the season bent, in the end, to a single unbending will. {pool:seasonClose}

{player} is champion, and the paddock knows a worthy one when it sees it. The {year} title goes to {team} on merit piled upon merit, a season of glamour and grease answered with a right foot that never once flinched. {pool:seasonClose}

Raise the glasses high: {player} has taken the {year} crown, and the {team} name goes into the book with the immortals. It was a summer of money and danger and impossible speed, and it belonged, when all was counted, to the best of them. {pool:seasonClose}

The last flag of {year} has fallen, and {player} stands above every rival — world champion, the {team} colours worn tonight more proudly than any silk in the paddock. A long season's reward, and richly deserved. {pool:seasonClose}
